name: environment-setup-guide
description: Guide developers through setting up development environments with proper tools, dependencies, and configurations
capabilities: Provide platform-specific installation instructions for macOS, Linux, and Windows
Guide users through checking existing tool versions before installation
Show how to configure environment variables and configuration files
Offer verification steps to confirm successful installation
Include troubleshooting for common development environment issues
Provide setup script templates for automation
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is documentation content providing development environment setup instructions. The static scanner flagged example shell commands and configuration examples as potential security issues, but these are standard instructional examples for installing software and configuring environments. No actual code execution or malicious patterns present - all findings are false positives from a documentation file.
valueStatement: This skill helps developers set up complete development environments from scratch with step-by-step guidance for installing tools, configuring dependencies, and verifying everything works correctly.
limitations: Does not automatically install software - provides guidance only
Does not execute commands on the user's system
Does not modify system configuration without user action
Does not provide interactive step-by-step installation

useCases.title: New Developer Onboarding
useCases.description: Help new team members set up their development environment with all required tools and dependencies

useCases.title: Machine Migration
useCases.description: Guide users through recreating their development environment on a new machine or operating system

useCases.title: Project Setup Documentation
useCases.description: Create clear setup instructions for team members to follow when starting a new project

promptTemplates.title: Basic Environment Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up a development environment for a Node.js project. I need to install Node.js, npm, Git, and Docker.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to set up basic tools for web development

promptTemplates.title: Python Environment
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to set up a Python development environment with Python 3.11, pip, virtual environments, and common packages like Flask and SQLAlchemy.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to set up Python-specific development environment

promptTemplates.title: Database Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up PostgreSQL and Redis for local development. Include configuration for environment variables and connection testing.
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs database systems for their development stack

promptTemplates.title: Full Stack Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete development environment setup guide for a MERN stack application. Include Node.js, MongoDB, React, and all required environment variables.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to set up a full-stack development environment

bestPractices: Always check existing tool versions before installing new ones to avoid conflicts
Use version managers like nvm for Node.js and pyenv for Python to manage multiple versions
Document all required environment variables using .env.example files
antiPatterns: Don't use sudo with npm install as it can cause permission issues
Don't skip environment variable configuration - always document required variables
Don't assume all team members have the same operating system - provide platform-specific instructions

faq.question: Does this skill install software on my computer?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides documentation and guidance. It shows you the commands to run, but you must execute them yourself in your terminal.

faq.question: Can this help me set up Docker?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes detailed instructions for installing Docker on macOS, Linux, and Windows, plus docker-compose setup examples.

faq.question: What programming languages does this cover?
faq.answer: The skill provides examples for Node.js, Python, Go, and general development environment setup. It includes platform-specific instructions for all major operating systems.

faq.question: How do I troubleshoot 'command not found' errors?
faq.answer: The skill includes a troubleshooting section covering common issues like PATH configuration, shell sourcing, and permission fixes.

faq.question: Can I use this for team onboarding?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is ideal for creating documentation to help new team members set up their development environments quickly.

faq.question: Does this work on Windows?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes Windows-specific instructions using Chocolatey and direct installers for all major tools.
